revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Minatoyama Onsen (Kobe)]][[ja:湊山温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Minatoyama Onsen (Kobe)]][[ja:湊山温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Minatoyama Onsen (Kobe)]][[ja:湊山温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11563139]] Wikidata name: Minatoyama Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Onsen in Japan | Name = Minatoyama Onsen | image = Minatoyama Spa.JPG | Comment = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|title= |marker= |zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|41|32.2|N|135|9|58.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward}}, Minatoyama-cho | Latitude = 34°41'32.2" N | Longitude = 135°9'58.6" E | Access = By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line}} (operated by {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. | Hot Spring Type = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} | Source Temperature = 28°C }}
[[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉C262973.jpg|thumb|240px|View from Onsen Bridge]]
'''Minatoyama Onsen''' (湊山温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Minatoyama-cho|ja|湊山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatoyama-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (formerly part of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}). The name "Minatoyama" comes from its location near {{ill|Minatogawa River|ja|湊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Minatogawa|WD=Q3314737}}, where the river emerges from the mountains into the plains. The spring water temperature is approximately 27°C, so it is heated using a gas-fired steam boiler to maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water.
== Hot Spring Qualities == * Contains carbon dioxide—sodium/calcium—bicarbonate/chloride spring (carbonated bicarbonate spring, low-temperature spring) with a source temperature of 28.2°C. The water is colorless at the source but develops a slight brown sediment when left standing. It has a hydrogen sulfide smell. * Source temperature: 28°C. The exact water output is unmeasurable due to multiple outflow points, but it is abundant relative to the facility size.
== Hot Spring Facility == [[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉前 C263014.jpg|thumb|230px|Retro Showa-era atmosphere near the entrance]]
The facility is a {{ill|day-use hot spring|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} with no lodging options, operated by Kobe Natural Mineral Springs. Although it resembles a {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=sento (Japanese public bath)}}, the admission fee is higher (as of August 2015: adults 680 yen, middle school students 430 yen, elementary school students 230 yen, infants (0-6 years) 110 yen).
Another day-use hot spring facility, "Tenno Onsen," was located about 50 meters to the south but closed in 2004 due to increased water fees following Kobe City's financial difficulties.
=== Bathing Areas === Despite the need for heating, all baths maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water. The baths include: * Cold bath with untreated spring water (~28°C) * Tepid bath (~38°C) * Jacuzzi (~38-41°C) * Main bath (~41-43°C) * High-temperature bath (~45-46°C)
The facility also features a drinking fountain using the natural spring water. Due to trace amounts of arsenic, the recommended daily drinking limit is 400 ml.
<gallery> File:湊山温泉温泉橋 minatoyama-onsen-bridgeC262970.JPG|Historic Onsen Bridge File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉下駄箱 C263002.jpg|Retro shoe lockers </gallery>
== History == Minatoyama Onsen is said to have existed since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, with legends claiming that aristocrats from the nearby {{ill|Fukuhara-kyō|en|Fukuhara-kyō|WD=Q6031707|lt=Fukuhara Palace}} and even [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}} bathed here, although there is no concrete evidence.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.daily.co.jp/society/life/2022/06/03/0015357574.shtml|title=Impactful Bath with 2000 Yellow Rubber Ducks at a Historic Hot Spring|publisher=Daily Sports Online|date=2022-06-03|accessdate=2022-06-03}}</ref>
The current Minatoyama Onsen facility opened in 1889, with the current building dating back to 1938. In the 1970s, it attracted 1,200–1,300 visitors daily, but numbers dropped to around 360 before its temporary closure in 2015. Due to increased operating costs, it was initially set to close on May 10, 2015,<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201504/0007892649.shtml Historic Hot Spring Linked to Taira no Kiyomori to Close - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, April 7, 2015]</ref> but after a brief hiatus, it was reopened under new management by Izu Kogen Beer Co. on May 14, 2015.<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201505/0007988840.shtml Historic Hot Spring Saved by Shizuoka Company - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, May 4, 2015]</ref>
== Access == * By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line}}, take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * Free parking available (20 spaces as of 2015).
== Location == * 26-1 Minatoyama-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture * Located just west of {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}
== Business Hours == * 5:00 AM – 11:30 PM (last admission at 11:00 PM), closed on Wednesdays (as of February 2020) * Entry Fees: ** Adults: 700 yen (550 yen from 5:00–8:00 AM) ** Elementary school students: 230 yen (100 yen on weekends) ** Infants (0–6 years): Free * Free body soap, shampoo, and conditioner provided * Rest area with free seating near the reception; paid 24-hour lounge on the second floor
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://minatoyama-onsen.com/ Official Minatoyama Onsen Website] * [http://maps.live.com/#JndoZXJlMT0lZTYlYjklOGElZTUlYjElYjElZTYlYjglYTklZTYlYjMlODkmYmI9MzQuNjkzNDAwMjIwMTQ2NSU3ZTEzNS4xNjc4MjU4MTgwNjIlN2UzNC42OTE0NjgzMjE0ODg1JTdlMTM1LjE2NDUxMDYwNzcxOQ== Minatoyama Onsen Map]
{{Onsen-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|湊山温泉|version=98849735|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Access == * By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line}}, take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * Free parking available (20 spaces as of 2015).
== Location == * 26-1 Minatoyama-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture * Located just west of {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}
== Business Hours == * 5:00 AM – 11:30 PM (last admission at 11:00 PM), closed on Wednesdays (as of February 2020) * Entry Fees: ** Adults: 700 yen (550 yen from 5:00–8:00 AM) ** Elementary school students: 230 yen (100 yen on weekends) ** Infants (0–6 years): Free * Free body soap, shampoo, and conditioner provided * Rest area with free seating near the reception; paid 24-hour lounge on the second floor
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://minatoyama-onsen.com/ Official Minatoyama Onsen Website] * [http://maps.live.com/#JndoZXJlMT0lZTYlYjklOGElZTUlYjElYjElZTYlYjglYTklZTYlYjMlODkmYmI9MzQuNjkzNDAwMjIwMTQ2NSU3ZTEzNS4xNjc4MjU4MTgwNjIlN2UzNC42OTE0NjgzMjE0ODg1JTdlMTM1LjE2NDUxMDYwNzcxOQ== Minatoyama Onsen Map]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Minatoyama Onsen (Kobe)]][[ja:湊山温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Minatoyama Onsen (Kobe)]][[ja:湊山温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Minatoyama Onsen (Kobe)]][[ja:湊山温泉]]
{{Onsen in Japan | Name = Minatoyama Onsen | image = Minatoyama Spa.JPG | Comment = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|title= |marker= |zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|41|32.2|N|135|9|58.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward}}, Minatoyama-cho | Latitude = 34°41'32.2" N | Longitude = 135°9'58.6" E | Access = By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line}} (operated by {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}), take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. | Hot Spring Type = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} | Source Temperature = 28°C }}
[[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉C262973.jpg|thumb|240px|View from Onsen Bridge]]
'''Minatoyama Onsen''' (湊山温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Minatoyama-cho|ja|湊山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatoyama-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (formerly part of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}). The name "Minatoyama" comes from its location near {{ill|Minatogawa River|ja|湊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Minatogawa|WD=Q3314737}}, where the river emerges from the mountains into the plains. The spring water temperature is approximately 27°C, so it is heated using a gas-fired steam boiler to maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water.
== Hot Spring Qualities == * Contains carbon dioxide—sodium/calcium—bicarbonate/chloride spring (carbonated bicarbonate spring, low-temperature spring) with a source temperature of 28.2°C. The water is colorless at the source but develops a slight brown sediment when left standing. It has a hydrogen sulfide smell. * Source temperature: 28°C. The exact water output is unmeasurable due to multiple outflow points, but it is abundant relative to the facility size.
== Hot Spring Facility == [[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉前 C263014.jpg|thumb|230px|Retro Showa-era atmosphere near the entrance]]
The facility is a {{ill|day-use hot spring|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} with no lodging options, operated by Kobe Natural Mineral Springs. Although it resembles a {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=sento (Japanese public bath)}}, the admission fee is higher (as of August 2015: adults 680 yen, middle school students 430 yen, elementary school students 230 yen, infants (0-6 years) 110 yen).
Another day-use hot spring facility, "Tenno Onsen," was located about 50 meters to the south but closed in 2004 due to increased water fees following Kobe City's financial difficulties.
=== Bathing Areas === Despite the need for heating, all baths maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water. The baths include: * Cold bath with untreated spring water (~28°C) * Tepid bath (~38°C) * Jacuzzi (~38-41°C) * Main bath (~41-43°C) * High-temperature bath (~45-46°C)
The facility also features a drinking fountain using the natural spring water. Due to trace amounts of arsenic, the recommended daily drinking limit is 400 ml.
<gallery> File:湊山温泉温泉橋 minatoyama-onsen-bridgeC262970.JPG|Historic Onsen Bridge File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉下駄箱 C263002.jpg|Retro shoe lockers </gallery>
== History == Minatoyama Onsen is said to have existed since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, with legends claiming that aristocrats from the nearby {{ill|Fukuhara-kyō|en|Fukuhara-kyō|WD=Q6031707|lt=Fukuhara Palace}} and even [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}} bathed here, although there is no concrete evidence.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.daily.co.jp/society/life/2022/06/03/0015357574.shtml|title=Impactful Bath with 2000 Yellow Rubber Ducks at a Historic Hot Spring|publisher=Daily Sports Online|date=2022-06-03|accessdate=2022-06-03}}</ref>
The current Minatoyama Onsen facility opened in 1889, with the current building dating back to 1938. In the 1970s, it attracted 1,200–1,300 visitors daily, but numbers dropped to around 360 before its temporary closure in 2015. Due to increased operating costs, it was initially set to close on May 10, 2015,<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201504/0007892649.shtml Historic Hot Spring Linked to Taira no Kiyomori to Close - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, April 7, 2015]</ref> but after a brief hiatus, it was reopened under new management by Izu Kogen Beer Co. on May 14, 2015.<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201505/0007988840.shtml Historic Hot Spring Saved by Shizuoka Company - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, May 4, 2015]</ref>
== Access == * By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line}}, take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * Free parking available (20 spaces as of 2015).
== Location == * 26-1 Minatoyama-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture * Located just west of {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrine|WD=Q11588442}}
== Business Hours == * 5:00 AM – 11:30 PM (last admission at 11:00 PM), closed on Wednesdays (as of February 2020) * Entry Fees: ** Adults: 700 yen (550 yen from 5:00–8:00 AM) ** Elementary school students: 230 yen (100 yen on weekends) ** Infants (0–6 years): Free * Free body soap, shampoo, and conditioner provided * Rest area with free seating near the reception; paid 24-hour lounge on the second floor
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://minatoyama-onsen.com/ Official Minatoyama Onsen Website] * [http://maps.live.com/#JndoZXJlMT0lZTYlYjklOGElZTUlYjElYjElZTYlYjglYTklZTYlYjMlODkmYmI9MzQuNjkzNDAwMjIwMTQ2NSU3ZTEzNS4xNjc4MjU4MTgwNjIlN2UzNC42OTE0NjgzMjE0ODg1JTdlMTM1LjE2NDUxMDYwNzcxOQ== Minatoyama Onsen Map]

The facility is a {{ill|day-use hot spring|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} with no lodging options, operated by Kobe Natural Mineral Springs. Although it resembles a {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=sento (Japanese public bath)|1=Sentō}}, the admission fee is higher (as of August 2015: adults 680 yen, middle school students 430 yen, elementary school students 230 yen, infants (0-6 years) 110 yen).
Another day-use hot spring facility, "Tenno Onsen," was located about 50 meters to the south but closed in 2004 due to increased water fees following Kobe City's financial difficulties.
=== Bathing Areas === Despite the need for heating, all baths maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water. The baths include: * Cold bath with untreated spring water (~28°C) * Tepid bath (~38°C) * Jacuzzi (~38-41°C) * Main bath (~41-43°C) * High-temperature bath (~45-46°C)
The facility also features a drinking fountain using the natural spring water. Due to trace amounts of arsenic, the recommended daily drinking limit is 400 ml.
<gallery> File:湊山温泉温泉橋 minatoyama-onsen-bridgeC262970.JPG|Historic Onsen Bridge File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉下駄箱 C263002.jpg|Retro shoe lockers </gallery>
== History == Minatoyama Onsen is said to have existed since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, with legends claiming that aristocrats from the nearby {{ill|Fukuhara-kyō|en|Fukuhara-kyō|WD=Q6031707|lt=Fukuhara Palace|1=Fukuhara-kyō}} and even [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}} bathed here, although there is no concrete evidence.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.daily.co.jp/society/life/2022/06/03/0015357574.shtml|title=Impactful Bath with 2000 Yellow Rubber Ducks at a Historic Hot Spring|publisher=Daily Sports Online|date=2022-06-03|accessdate=2022-06-03}}</ref>
The current Minatoyama Onsen facility opened in 1889, with the current building dating back to 1938. In the 1970s, it attracted 1,200–1,300 visitors daily, but numbers dropped to around 360 before its temporary closure in 2015. Due to increased operating costs, it was initially set to close on May 10, 2015,<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201504/0007892649.shtml Historic Hot Spring Linked to Taira no Kiyomori to Close - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, April 7, 2015]</ref> but after a brief hiatus, it was reopened under new management by Izu Kogen Beer Co. on May 14, 2015.<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201505/0007988840.shtml Historic Hot Spring Saved by Shizuoka Company - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, May 4, 2015]</ref>
== Access == * By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)|1=神戸駅}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)|1=Motomachi Station}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line|1=JR Kōbe Line}}, take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * Free parking available (20 spaces as of 2015).
== Location == * 26-1 Minatoyama-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture * Located just west of {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}
== Business Hours == * 5:00 AM – 11:30 PM (last admission at 11:00 PM), closed on Wednesdays (as of February 2020) * Entry Fees: ** Adults: 700 yen (550 yen from 5:00–8:00 AM) ** Elementary school students: 230 yen (100 yen on weekends) ** Infants (0–6 years): Free * Free body soap, shampoo, and conditioner provided * Rest area with free seating near the reception; paid 24-hour lounge on the second floor

[[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉C262973.jpg|thumb|240px|View from Onsen Bridge]]
'''Minatoyama Onsen''' (湊山温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Minatoyama-cho|ja|湊山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatoyama-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (formerly part of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}). The name "Minatoyama" comes from its location near {{ill|Minatogawa River|ja|湊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Minatogawa|WD=Q3314737}}, where the river emerges from the mountains into the plains. The spring water temperature is approximately 27°C, so it is heated using a gas-fired steam boiler to maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water.
== Hot Spring Qualities == * Contains carbon dioxide—sodium/calcium—bicarbonate/chloride spring (carbonated bicarbonate spring, low-temperature spring) with a source temperature of 28.2°C. The water is colorless at the source but develops a slight brown sediment when left standing. It has a hydrogen sulfide smell. * Source temperature: 28°C. The exact water output is unmeasurable due to multiple outflow points, but it is abundant relative to the facility size.
== Hot Spring Facility == [[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉前 C263014.jpg|thumb|230px|Retro Showa-era atmosphere near the entrance]]
The facility is a {{ill|day-use hot spring|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} with no lodging options, operated by Kobe Natural Mineral Springs. Although it resembles a {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=sento (Japanese public bath)|1=Sentō}}, the admission fee is higher (as of August 2015: adults 680 yen, middle school students 430 yen, elementary school students 230 yen, infants (0-6 years) 110 yen).
Another day-use hot spring facility, "Tenno Onsen," was located about 50 meters to the south but closed in 2004 due to increased water fees following Kobe City's financial difficulties.
=== Bathing Areas === Despite the need for heating, all baths maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water. The baths include: * Cold bath with untreated spring water (~28°C) * Tepid bath (~38°C) * Jacuzzi (~38-41°C) * Main bath (~41-43°C) * High-temperature bath (~45-46°C)
The facility also features a drinking fountain using the natural spring water. Due to trace amounts of arsenic, the recommended daily drinking limit is 400 ml.

{{Onsen in Japan | Name = Minatoyama Onsen | image = Minatoyama Spa.JPG | Comment = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|title= |marker= |zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|41|32.2|N|135|9|58.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, Minatoyama-cho | Latitude = 34°41'32.2" N | Longitude = 135°9'58.6" E | Access = By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)|1=神戸駅}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)|1=Motomachi Station}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line|1=JR Kōbe Line}} (operated by {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}), take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. | Hot Spring Type = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} | Source Temperature = 28°C }}
[[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉C262973.jpg|thumb|240px|View from Onsen Bridge]]
'''Minatoyama Onsen''' (湊山温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Minatoyama-cho|ja|湊山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatoyama-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (formerly part of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}). The name "Minatoyama" comes from its location near {{ill|Minatogawa River|ja|湊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Minatogawa|WD=Q3314737}}, where the river emerges from the mountains into the plains. The spring water temperature is approximately 27°C, so it is heated using a gas-fired steam boiler to maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water.
== Hot Spring Qualities == * Contains carbon dioxide—sodium/calcium—bicarbonate/chloride spring (carbonated bicarbonate spring, low-temperature spring) with a source temperature of 28.2°C. The water is colorless at the source but develops a slight brown sediment when left standing. It has a hydrogen sulfide smell. * Source temperature: 28°C. The exact water output is unmeasurable due to multiple outflow points, but it is abundant relative to the facility size.
== Hot Spring Facility == [[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉前 C263014.jpg|thumb|230px|Retro Showa-era atmosphere near the entrance]]
The facility is a {{ill|day-use hot spring|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} with no lodging options, operated by Kobe Natural Mineral Springs. Although it resembles a {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=sento (Japanese public bath)|1=Sentō}}, the admission fee is higher (as of August 2015: adults 680 yen, middle school students 430 yen, elementary school students 230 yen, infants (0-6 years) 110 yen).
Another day-use hot spring facility, "Tenno Onsen," was located about 50 meters to the south but closed in 2004 due to increased water fees following Kobe City's financial difficulties.
=== Bathing Areas === Despite the need for heating, all baths maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water. The baths include: * Cold bath with untreated spring water (~28°C) * Tepid bath (~38°C) * Jacuzzi (~38-41°C) * Main bath (~41-43°C) * High-temperature bath (~45-46°C)
The facility also features a drinking fountain using the natural spring water. Due to trace amounts of arsenic, the recommended daily drinking limit is 400 ml.
<gallery> File:湊山温泉温泉橋 minatoyama-onsen-bridgeC262970.JPG|Historic Onsen Bridge File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉下駄箱 C263002.jpg|Retro shoe lockers </gallery>
== History == Minatoyama Onsen is said to have existed since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, with legends claiming that aristocrats from the nearby {{ill|Fukuhara-kyō|en|Fukuhara-kyō|WD=Q6031707|lt=Fukuhara Palace|1=Fukuhara-kyō}} and even [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}} bathed here, although there is no concrete evidence.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.daily.co.jp/society/life/2022/06/03/0015357574.shtml|title=Impactful Bath with 2000 Yellow Rubber Ducks at a Historic Hot Spring|publisher=Daily Sports Online|date=2022-06-03|accessdate=2022-06-03}}</ref>
The current Minatoyama Onsen facility opened in 1889, with the current building dating back to 1938. In the 1970s, it attracted 1,200–1,300 visitors daily, but numbers dropped to around 360 before its temporary closure in 2015. Due to increased operating costs, it was initially set to close on May 10, 2015,<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201504/0007892649.shtml Historic Hot Spring Linked to Taira no Kiyomori to Close - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, April 7, 2015]</ref> but after a brief hiatus, it was reopened under new management by Izu Kogen Beer Co. on May 14, 2015.<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201505/0007988840.shtml Historic Hot Spring Saved by Shizuoka Company - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, May 4, 2015]</ref>
== Access == * By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|WD=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)|1=神戸駅}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|WD=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)|1=Motomachi Station}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|WD=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line|1=JR Kōbe Line}}, take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * Free parking available (20 spaces as of 2015).
== Location == * 26-1 Minatoyama-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture * Located just west of {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|WD=Q11588442}}
== Business Hours == * 5:00 AM – 11:30 PM (last admission at 11:00 PM), closed on Wednesdays (as of February 2020) * Entry Fees: ** Adults: 700 yen (550 yen from 5:00–8:00 AM) ** Elementary school students: 230 yen (100 yen on weekends) ** Infants (0–6 years): Free * Free body soap, shampoo, and conditioner provided * Rest area with free seating near the reception; paid 24-hour lounge on the second floor
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://minatoyama-onsen.com/ Official Minatoyama Onsen Website] * [http://maps.live.com/#JndoZXJlMT0lZTYlYjklOGElZTUlYjElYjElZTYlYjglYTklZTYlYjMlODkmYmI9MzQuNjkzNDAwMjIwMTQ2NSU3ZTEzNS4xNjc4MjU4MTgwNjIlN2UzNC42OTE0NjgzMjE0ODg1JTdlMTM1LjE2NDUxMDYwNzcxOQ== Minatoyama Onsen Map]
{{Onsen-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|湊山温泉|version=98849735|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Due to increased operating costs, it was initially set to close on May 10, 2015,<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201504/0007892649.shtml Historic Hot Spring Linked to Taira no Kiyomori to Close - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, April 7, 2015]</ref> but after a brief hiatus, it was reopened under new management by Izu Kogen Beer Co. on May 14, 2015.<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201505/0007988840.shtml Historic Hot Spring Saved by Shizuoka Company - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, May 4, 2015]</ref>
== Access == * By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|qid=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)|1=神戸駅}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|qid=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)|1=Motomachi Station}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|qid=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line|1=JR Kōbe Line}}, take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * Free parking available (20 spaces as of 2015).
== Location == * 26-1 Minatoyama-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture * Located just west of {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|qid=Q11588442}}
== Business Hours == * 5:00 AM – 11:30 PM (last admission at 11:00 PM), closed on Wednesdays (as of February 2020) * Entry Fees: ** Adults: 700 yen (550 yen from 5:00–8:00 AM) ** Elementary school students: 230 yen (100 yen on weekends) ** Infants (0–6 years): Free * Free body soap, shampoo, and conditioner provided * Rest area with free seating near the reception; paid 24-hour lounge on the second floor

{{Onsen in Japan | Name = Minatoyama Onsen | image = Minatoyama Spa.JPG | Comment = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|title= |marker= |zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|41|32.2|N|135|9|58.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, Minatoyama-cho | Latitude = 34°41'32.2" N | Longitude = 135°9'58.6" E | Access = By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|qid=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)|1=神戸駅}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|qid=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)|1=Motomachi Station}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|qid=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line|1=JR Kōbe Line}} (operated by {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}), take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. | Hot Spring Type = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|qid=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} | Source Temperature = 28°C }}
[[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉C262973.jpg|thumb|240px|View from Onsen Bridge]]
'''Minatoyama Onsen''' (湊山温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Minatoyama-cho|ja|湊山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatoyama-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} (formerly part of {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}). The name "Minatoyama" comes from its location near {{ill|Minatogawa River|ja|湊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Minatogawa|qid=Q3314737}}, where the river emerges from the mountains into the plains. The spring water temperature is approximately 27°C, so it is heated using a gas-fired steam boiler to maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water.
== Hot Spring Qualities == * Contains carbon dioxide—sodium/calcium—bicarbonate/chloride spring (carbonated bicarbonate spring, low-temperature spring) with a source temperature of 28.2°C. The water is colorless at the source but develops a slight brown sediment when left standing. It has a hydrogen sulfide smell. * Source temperature: 28°C. The exact water output is unmeasurable due to multiple outflow points, but it is abundant relative to the facility size.
== Hot Spring Facility == [[File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉前 C263014.jpg|thumb|230px|Retro Showa-era atmosphere near the entrance]]
The facility is a {{ill|day-use hot spring|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} with no lodging options, operated by Kobe Natural Mineral Springs. Although it resembles a {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|qid=Q1046648|lt=sento (Japanese public bath)|1=Sentō}}, the admission fee is higher (as of August 2015: adults 680 yen, middle school students 430 yen, elementary school students 230 yen, infants (0-6 years) 110 yen).
Another day-use hot spring facility, "Tenno Onsen," was located about 50 meters to the south but closed in 2004 due to increased water fees following Kobe City's financial difficulties.
=== Bathing Areas === Despite the need for heating, all baths maintain a continuous flow of natural hot spring water. The baths include: * Cold bath with untreated spring water (~28°C) * Tepid bath (~38°C) * Jacuzzi (~38-41°C) * Main bath (~41-43°C) * High-temperature bath (~45-46°C)
The facility also features a drinking fountain using the natural spring water. Due to trace amounts of arsenic, the recommended daily drinking limit is 400 ml.
<gallery> File:湊山温泉温泉橋 minatoyama-onsen-bridgeC262970.JPG|Historic Onsen Bridge File:Minatoyama-onsen 湊山温泉下駄箱 C263002.jpg|Retro shoe lockers </gallery>
== History == Minatoyama Onsen is said to have existed since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, with legends claiming that aristocrats from the nearby {{ill|Fukuhara-kyō|en|Fukuhara-kyō|qid=Q6031707|lt=Fukuhara Palace|1=Fukuhara-kyō}} and even [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}} bathed here, although there is no concrete evidence.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.daily.co.jp/society/life/2022/06/03/0015357574.shtml|title=Impactful Bath with 2000 Yellow Rubber Ducks at a Historic Hot Spring|publisher=Daily Sports Online|date=2022-06-03|accessdate=2022-06-03}}</ref>
The current Minatoyama Onsen facility opened in 1889, with the current building dating back to 1938. In the 1970s, it attracted 1,200–1,300 visitors daily, but numbers dropped to around 360 before its temporary closure in 2015. Due to increased operating costs, it was initially set to close on May 10, 2015,<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201504/0007892649.shtml Historic Hot Spring Linked to Taira no Kiyomori to Close - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, April 7, 2015]</ref> but after a brief hiatus, it was reopened under new management by Izu Kogen Beer Co. on May 14, 2015.<ref>[http://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/shakai/201505/0007988840.shtml Historic Hot Spring Saved by Shizuoka Company - Kobe Shimbun NEXT, May 4, 2015]</ref>
== Access == * By train: From {{ill|神戸駅|en|神戸駅|qid=Q22341570|lt=Kobe Station (Hyōgo)|1=神戸駅}} or {{ill|Motomachi Station|en|Motomachi Station|qid=Q847752|lt=Motomachi Station (Hyōgo)|1=Motomachi Station}} on the {{ill|JR Kōbe Line|en|JR Kōbe Line|qid=Q613302|lt=JR Kobe Line|1=JR Kōbe Line}}, take a {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神戸市バス|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589912}} to Hirano Bus Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * Free parking available (20 spaces as of 2015).
== Location == * 26-1 Minatoyama-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture * Located just west of {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|qid=Q11588442}}
== Business Hours == * 5:00 AM – 11:30 PM (last admission at 11:00 PM), closed on Wednesdays (as of February 2020) * Entry Fees: ** Adults: 700 yen (550 yen from 5:00–8:00 AM) ** Elementary school students: 230 yen (100 yen on weekends) ** Infants (0–6 years): Free * Free body soap, shampoo, and conditioner provided * Rest area with free seating near the reception; paid 24-hour lounge on the second floor
